title: Koeleria obscura
taxonomy: Koeleria obscura is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It belongs to the tribe Poeae and is a member of the subfamily Pooideae. The genus Koeleria is a group of grasses that are native to the Northern Hemisphere, with Koeleria obscura being one of the species within this genus. Koeleria obscura is also known as the lesser June-grass, which is a name that reflects its flowering period. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Koeleria obscura.

intro: Koeleria obscura, also known as the Purple Hair Grass or Black Needle Grass, is a species of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, as it has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote the expulsion of mucus from the lungs. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria obscura include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Koeleria obscura is often made into herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems.
